```{r GOEAmap, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
p2 <- plot_raster(x = paste0("data/gam/ACP-", spp, "-2026-04-06.tif"), 
                  what = "gg", log = TRUE)

p1/p2 + plot_annotation(tag_levels = 'A')
```

{{< pagebreak >}}

## Short-eared Owl {.unnumbered}

```{r SEOWcap, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
 cap = paste0("Short-eared owl (*Asio flammeus*) indices of breeding birds (grey circles; singles + [2 x pairs]) and total birds (black circles; singles + [2 x pairs] + birds in flocks) with 95% confidence intervals (vertical bars from circles), as well as the long-term trend in total birds (black line; 2007â€“",max(ACPHistoric$combined$Year),") and associated 95% credible intervals (grey band around line) from the Arctic Coastal Plain Aerial Breeding Population Survey, Alaska, 2007â€“",max(ACPHistoric$combined$Year),". Years with counts of zero were not included in trend estimates (see Methods for details).")
```

```{r SEOWfig, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
#| label: fig-SEOW
#| fig-cap: !expr cap
#| fig-alt: A graph of the average index estimate between observers (vertical bars from circles), the posterior mean population growth trajectory 2007-2025 (black line), and the approximate 95% confidence interval for the trajectory (grey band around line).
 
spp = "SEOW"
df <- AKaerial::ACPHistoric$combined |>
  filter(Species == spp) |>
  select(Year, index = total, se = total.se, index2 = sing1pair2, se2 = sing1pair2.se) |> 
  mutate(upper = index +1.96*se, lower = index - 1.96*se, upper2 = index2+1.96*se2, 
         lower2 = index2 - 1.96*se2)
df2 <- read_csv(file = paste0("data/plot_trends/data/",spp,"_bootfit.csv"))
ggplot(data = df2) + 
  geom_ribbon(aes(x=Year, ymin=pmax(0, lower), ymax = upper), fill = "lightgray", 
              alpha = 0.5) + 
  geom_line(aes(x=Year, y=mean), lwd = 1) + 
  geom_pointrange(data = df, aes(x=Year-0.1, y=index, ymin=pmax(0,lower), ymax = upper, 
                  col = "Total")) + 
  geom_pointrange(data = df, aes(x=Year+0.1, y=index2, ymin=pmax(0,lower2), ymax = upper2, 
                  col = "Breeding")) +
  labs(x = "Year", y = "Population Index") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(2007, 2025, by = 2)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
  scale_colour_manual(values=c("Total"= "black", "Breeding" = "darkgray")) +  
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(legend.position = "top", legend.title=element_blank(), legend.text=element_text(size=11)) +
  theme(text=element_text(family="Times")) +
  theme(axis.title=element_text(size=13,face="bold")) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size=11), axis.text.y = element_text(size=11))

```

{{< pagebreak >}}

## Short-eared Owl {.unnumbered}

```{r SEOW, include=TRUE, echo=FALSE}
#| label: tbl-SEOW
cap <- paste0("Short-eared owl (*Asio flammeus*) indices of breeding birds (singles + [2 x pairs]) and total birds (singles + [2 x pairs] + birds in flocks) with standard errors (SE) from the Arctic Coastal Plain Aerial Breeding Population Survey, Alaska, 2007-",max(ACPHistoric$combined$Year),".")

kable(SEOW, digits = 0, booktabs = TRUE, linesep = "", longtable=TRUE, format.args = list(big.mark = ","), caption = cap, align = "lrrrr", col.names = c("Year", "Breeding Birds", "SE", "Total Birds", "SE")) %>%
  kable_styling(font_size=10, position="center")%>%
  row_spec(row = 0, bold = TRUE)

```

{{< pagebreak >}}

## Short-eared Owl {.unnumbered}

```{r SEOWmcap, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
 cap = paste0("(A) Observations of short-eared owls (*Asio flammeus*) along transects within four physiographic-based strata (high, medium, low, and Teshekpuk high) from the Arctic Coastal Plain Aerial Breeding Population Survey, Alaska, during the most recent survey year (",max(ACPHistoric$combined$Year),"). (B) Expected density (2007â€“",max(ACPHistoric$combined$Year),") of indicated breeding pairs (number of single birds + number of bird pairs) on the natural log scale across the survey area predicted from a generalized additive model after removing observer, year, and spatio-temporal effects. The scale bar shows density of indicated breeding pairs per square kilometer from the minimum to maximum value for this species." )
```

```{r SEOWmap, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
#| label: fig-SEOWmap
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 7
#| fig-cap: !expr cap
#| fig-alt: In (A) is a map of species-specific observation locations (black dots) along transects within four physiographic-based strata (high, medium, low, and Teshekpuk high) of the Arctic Coastal Aerial Plain Breeding Population Survey, Alaska, during the most recent survey year. In (B) is a map of expected density (all survey years) of indicated breeding pairs (number of single birds + number of bird pairs) on the natural log scale across the survey area predicted from a generalized additive model after removing observer, year, and spatio-temporal effects. The scale bar shows density of indicated breeding pairs per square kilometer from the minimum to maximum value for this species.  

p1 <- AKaerial::ObsMap(area="ACP", species=spp) + coord_sf(crs = 3338)

p2 <- plot_raster(x = paste0("data/gam/ACP-", spp, "-2026-04-06.tif"), 
                  what = "gg", log = TRUE)

p1/p2 + plot_annotation(tag_levels = 'A')
```

{{< pagebreak >}}

## Snowy Owl {.unnumbered}

```{r SNOWcap, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
 cap = paste0("Snowy owl (*Bubo scandiacus*) indices of breeding birds (grey circles; singles + [2 x pairs]) and total birds (black circles; singles + [2 x pairs] + birds in flocks) with 95% confidence intervals (vertical bars from circles), as well as the long-term trend in total birds (black line; 2007â€“",max(ACPHistoric$combined$Year),") and associated 95% credible intervals (grey band around line) from the Arctic Coastal Plain Aerial Breeding Population Survey, Alaska, 2007â€“",max(ACPHistoric$combined$Year),". Years with counts of zero were not included in trend estimates (see Methods for details).")
```

```{r SNOWfig, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
#| label: fig-SNOW
#| fig-cap: !expr cap
#| fig-alt: A graph of the average index estimate between observers (vertical bars from circles), the posterior mean population growth trajectory 2007-2025 (black line), and the approximate 95% confidence interval for the trajectory (grey band around line).
 
spp = "SNOW"
df <- AKaerial::ACPHistoric$combined |>
  filter(Species == spp) |>
  select(Year, index = total, se = total.se, index2 = sing1pair2, se2 = sing1pair2.se) |> 
  mutate(upper = index +1.96*se, lower = index - 1.96*se, upper2 = index2+1.96*se2, 
         lower2 = index2 - 1.96*se2)
df2 <- read_csv(file = paste0("data/plot_trends/data/",spp,"_bootfit.csv"))
ggplot(data = df2) + 
  geom_ribbon(aes(x=Year, ymin=pmax(0, lower), ymax = upper), fill = "lightgray", 
              alpha = 0.5) + 
  geom_line(aes(x=Year, y=mean), lwd = 1) + 
  geom_pointrange(data = df, aes(x=Year-0.1, y=index, ymin=pmax(0,lower), ymax = upper, 
                  col = "Total")) + 
  geom_pointrange(data = df, aes(x=Year+0.1, y=index2, ymin=pmax(0,lower2), ymax = upper2, 
                  col = "Breeding")) +
  labs(x = "Year", y = "Population Index") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(2007, 2025, by = 2)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
  scale_colour_manual(values=c("Total"= "black", "Breeding" = "darkgray")) +  
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(legend.position = "top", legend.title=element_blank(), legend.text=element_text(size=11)) +
  theme(text=element_text(family="Times")) +
  theme(axis.title=element_text(size=13,face="bold")) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size=11), axis.text.y = element_text(size=11))

```

{{< pagebreak >}}

## Snowy Owl {.unnumbered}

```{r SNOW, include=TRUE, echo=FALSE}
#| label: tbl-SNOW
cap <- paste0("Snowy owl (*Bubo scandiacus*) indices of breeding birds (singles + [2 x pairs]) and total birds (singles + [2 x pairs] + birds in flocks) with standard errors (SE) from the Arctic Coastal Plain Aerial Breeding Population Survey, Alaska, 2007-",max(ACPHistoric$combined$Year),".")

kable(SNOW, digits = 0, booktabs = TRUE, linesep = "", longtable=TRUE, format.args = list(big.mark = ","), caption = cap, align = "lrrrr", col.names = c("Year", "Breeding Birds", "SE", "Total Birds", "SE")) %>%
  kable_styling(font_size=10, position="center")%>%
  row_spec(row = 0, bold = TRUE)

```

{{< pagebreak >}}

## Snowy Owl {.unnumbered}

```{r SNOWmcap, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
 cap = paste0("(A) Observations of snowy owls (*Bubo scandiacus*) along transects within four physiographic-based strata (high, medium, low, and Teshekpuk high) from the Arctic Coastal Plain Aerial Breeding Population Survey, Alaska, during the most recent survey year (",max(ACPHistoric$combined$Year),"). (B) Expected density (2007â€“",max(ACPHistoric$combined$Year),") of indicated breeding pairs (number of single birds + number of bird pairs) on the natural log scale across the survey area predicted from a generalized additive model after removing observer, year, and spatio-temporal effects. The scale bar shows density of indicated breeding pairs per square kilometer from the minimum to maximum value for this species." )
```

```{r SNOWmap, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
#| label: fig-SNOWmap
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 7
#| fig-cap: !expr cap
#| fig-alt: In (A) is a map of species-specific observation locations (black dots) along transects within four physiographic-based strata (high, medium, low, and Teshekpuk high) of the Arctic Coastal Aerial Plain Breeding Population Survey, Alaska, during the most recent survey year. In (B) is a map of expected density (all survey years) of indicated breeding pairs (number of single birds + number of bird pairs) on the natural log scale across the survey area predicted from a generalized additive model after removing observer, year, and spatio-temporal effects. The scale bar shows density of indicated breeding pairs per square kilometer from the minimum to maximum value for this species.  

p1 <- AKaerial::ObsMap(area="ACP", species=spp) + coord_sf(crs = 3338)

p2 <- plot_raster(x = paste0("data/gam/ACP-", spp, "-2026-04-06.tif"), 
                  what = "gg", log = TRUE)

p1/p2 + plot_annotation(tag_levels = 'A')
```

{{< pagebreak >}}

## Common Raven {.unnumbered}

```{r CORAcap, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
 cap = paste0("Common raven (*Corvus corax*) indices of breeding birds (grey circles; singles + [2 x pairs]) and total birds (black circles; singles + [2 x pairs] + birds in flocks) with 95% confidence intervals (vertical bars from circles), as well as the long-term trend in total birds (black line; 2007â€“",max(ACPHistoric$combined$Year),") and associated 95% credible intervals (grey band around line) from the Arctic Coastal Plain Aerial Breeding Population Survey, Alaska, 2007â€“",max(ACPHistoric$combined$Year),". Years with counts of zero were not included in trend estimates (see Methods for details).")
```

```{r CORAfig, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
#| label: fig-CORA
#| fig-cap: !expr cap
#| fig-alt: A graph of the average index estimate between observers (vertical bars from circles), the posterior mean population growth trajectory 2007-2025 (black line), and the approximate 95% confidence interval for the trajectory (grey band around line).
 
spp = "CORA"
df <- AKaerial::ACPHistoric$combined |>
  filter(Species == spp) |>
  select(Year, index = total, se = total.se, index2 = sing1pair2, se2 = sing1pair2.se) |> 
  mutate(upper = index +1.96*se, lower = index - 1.96*se, upper2 = index2+1.96*se2, 
         lower2 = index2 - 1.96*se2)
df2 <- read_csv(file = paste0("data/plot_trends/data/",spp,"_bootfit.csv"))
ggplot(data = df2) + 
  geom_ribbon(aes(x=Year, ymin=pmax(0, lower), ymax = upper), fill = "lightgray", 
              alpha = 0.5) + 
  geom_line(aes(x=Year, y=mean), lwd = 1) + 
  geom_pointrange(data = df, aes(x=Year-0.1, y=index, ymin=pmax(0,lower), ymax = upper, 
                  col = "Total")) + 
  geom_pointrange(data = df, aes(x=Year+0.1, y=index2, ymin=pmax(0,lower2), ymax = upper2, 
                  col = "Breeding")) +
  labs(x = "Year", y = "Population Index") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(2007, 2025, by = 2)) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
  scale_colour_manual(values=c("Total"= "black", "Breeding" = "darkgray")) +  
  theme_bw() + 
  theme(legend.position = "top", legend.title=element_blank(), legend.text=element_text(size=11)) +
  theme(text=element_text(family="Times")) +
  theme(axis.title=element_text(size=13,face="bold")) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(size=11), axis.text.y = element_text(size=11))

```

{{< pagebreak >}}

## Common Raven {.unnumbered}

```{r CORA, include=TRUE, echo=FALSE}
#| label: tbl-CORA
cap <- paste0("Common raven (*Corvus corax*) indices of breeding birds (singles + [2 x pairs]) and total birds (singles + [2 x pairs] + birds in flocks) with standard errors (SE) from the Arctic Coastal Plain Aerial Breeding Population Survey, Alaska, 2007-",max(ACPHistoric$combined$Year),".")

kable(CORA, digits = 0, booktabs = TRUE, linesep = "", longtable=TRUE, format.args = list(big.mark = ","), caption = cap, align = "lrrrr", col.names = c("Year", "Breeding Birds", "SE", "Total Birds", "SE")) %>%
  kable_styling(font_size=10, position="center")%>%
  row_spec(row = 0, bold = TRUE)

```

{{< pagebreak >}}

## Common Raven {.unnumbered}

```{r CORAmcap, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
 cap = paste0("(A) Observations of common ravens (*Corvus corax*) along transects within four physiographic-based strata (high, medium, low, and Teshekpuk high) from the Arctic Coastal Plain Aerial Breeding Population Survey, Alaska, during the most recent survey year (",max(ACPHistoric$combined$Year),"). (B) Expected density (2007â€“",max(ACPHistoric$combined$Year),") of indicated breeding pairs (number of single birds + number of bird pairs) on the natural log scale across the survey area predicted from a generalized additive model after removing observer, year, and spatio-temporal effects. The scale bar shows density of indicated breeding pairs per square kilometer from the minimum to maximum value for this species." )
```

```{r CORAmap, echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
#| label: fig-CORAmap
#| fig-width: 7
#| fig-height: 7
#| fig-cap: !expr cap
#| fig-alt: In (A) is a map of species-specific observation locations (black dots) along transects within four physiographic-based strata (high, medium, low, and Teshekpuk high) of the Arctic Coastal Aerial Plain Breeding Population Survey, Alaska, during the most recent survey year. In (B) is a map of expected density (all survey years) of indicated breeding pairs (number of single birds + number of bird pairs) on the natural log scale across the survey area predicted from a generalized additive model after removing observer, year, and spatio-temporal effects. The scale bar shows density of indicated breeding pairs per square kilometer from the minimum to maximum value for this species.  

p1 <- AKaerial::ObsMap(area="ACP", species=spp) + coord_sf(crs = 3338)

p2 <- plot_raster(x = paste0("data/gam/ACP-", spp, "-2026-04-06.tif"), 
                  what = "gg", log = TRUE)

p1/p2 + plot_annotation(tag_levels = 'A')
```
